preamble: This phonetic shift, known as depalatalization, involves the backing of an alveolo-palatal affricate to a velar plosive, often accompanied by de-affrication. It represents a reversal of the more common palatalization process where velar consonants move forward to become palatal or alveolo-palatal.

examples.note: The morpheme 'give' in the Beijing dialect underwent a depalatalization from an alveolo-palatal affricate [tɕ] to a velar plosive [k]. This shift started in the early 19th century and was established by the mid-19th century.

sources: LIU, S. (2021). The Sound Change from ［ʨi］ to ［kei］ of the Morpheme ＇Give＇（給）: Its Timeline and Explanation. Fukuoka University Review of Literature & Humanities, 53(1), 75-124.
